content:
Hello, just reposting this from Reddit:

“Hi, I'm interested in trying to write a simple Rust kernel module, however every example I've found online, other than the very simple Hello World style one, seems extremely out of date, specifically it seems "kernel::file" and "kernel::miscdev" are no longer a part of the kernel crate (https:// rust.docs.kernel.org/kernel/) yet everywhere | look that's what people use to attach to file openings and for registering device files. Also the documentation for the "kernel::device" crate is basically just: "go see the C.h file" so if anyone knows better places to find good examples or documentation that would be great.”

I see the miscdev abstractions seem to be landing in v6.13 however in the meantime are there any resources to work around this stuff?
Both file and miscdev are landing for 6.13. You could just use those?
Thanks I guess a follow up question is why were they removed? Compatibility issues I assume?
They were not removed, but rather they were never upstreamed :) i.e. the project started out-of-tree, and like other kernel projects, we are upstreaming as it is needed for users in mainline.
